name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
notes: Must score at least 55% in Class 7 (relaxable by 5% for SC/ST). Only for govt/govt-aided/local body schools - not KV, Navodaya, or private schools. Must maintain 60% in Class 10 and clear promotion each year to continue.

name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girls
benefits: ?50,000/yr, up to 3 yrs
notes: Maximum 2 girls per family. Admission must be through centralised State/Central counselling at an AICTE-approved institute, not management quota. Apply via National Scholarship Portal.

name: AICTE Saksham Scholarship for Specially-Abled
notes: Requires a minimum 40% disability certified by a government medical board (private hospital/individual doctor certificates not accepted). Management quota admissions are not eligible.

name: PM-YASASVI Top Class School (OBC/EBC/DNT)
notes: Selection method has shifted toward merit on qualifying-class marks (the older YASASVI Entrance Test was cancelled for recent cycles) - confirm current method on NSP before assuming.

name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship (CSSS)
benefits: ?10-20k/yr by level
notes: Must be in the top 20th percentile of your board's Class 12 results. Not available for diploma, distance, or correspondence courses. 50% of scholarships reserved for girls.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
benefits: Fee reimbursement + maintenance allowance
notes: Must be renewed every academic year. Apply via NSP or your state's SC scholarship portal. Aadhaar-linked bank account required for DBT

name: Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship
benefits: ~?5k/yr (9-10), ~?6k/yr (11-12)  unverified, sources disagreed
notes: Apply separately at maef.nic.in, not always bundled with main NSP flow. Benefit amount had the most source disagreement of all 12 - verify on official site before finalizing.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
benefits: Maintenance + fee reimbursement, varies
notes: Certificate must explicitly say 'Non-Creamy Layer', not just OBC. Income ceiling appears to have been revised upward to 2.5 lakh in recent cycles - older sources still cite 1.5 lakh, verify current figure.

name: INSPIRE Scholarship (SHE)
benefits: ?80,000/yr for 5 yrs
notes: No income criteria - purely merit/rank based. NOT for Engineering, Medicine, or other professional courses. Eligible via top 1% in Class 12 boards, top 10,000 JEE/NEET rank, IISER/NISER admission, or KVPY/NTSE/JBNSTS/Olympiad medal.

name: Swami Vivekananda Merit-cum-Means (SVMCM)
notes: Only for students who are BOTH domiciled in and studying within West Bengal. Minimum marks requirement varies by level (60% is the most commonly cited figure for HS/UG) - confirm exact percentage for your specific course on the portal. Cannot be combined with Kanyashree K3 for the same course.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for ST Students
benefits: Fee reimbursement + ?225/?525 per month
notes: Implemented by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs (separate from the SC/OBC schemes run by Social Justice ministry). Funds shared 75:25 between Centre and State (90:10 for NE/special category states).

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Minorities
notes: Scope appears to have narrowed in recent cycles from Class 1-10 down to mainly Class 9-10 - this had the second-most source disagreement, verify current class range on NSP. Max 2 children per family eligible; 30% of slots reserved for girls.

name: Ayushman Bharat  Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY)
max_income: No fixed cap for SECC-listed deprivation/occupational categories; families with annual income up to ?5 lakh may also qualify under some state expansion
benefits: Free health insurance up to ?5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization, cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals, no limit on family size, age, or gender, pre-existing diseases covered from day one; ManipalCigna senior citizens 70+ get an additional ?5 lakh top-up via Ayushman Vay Vandana Card regardless of income
notes: Eligibility based on SECC 2011 data; now operational across all 28 states and 8 UTs as of June 2026, with West Bengal joining June 8, 2026.

name: Central Government Health Scheme (CGHS)
max_income: No income limit (contribution scaled by pay/pension)
benefits: OPD consultations, inpatient hospitalisation, AYUSH treatments, maternity and child care, cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals; HDFC ERGO coverage up to ?5 lakh per family per year
notes: Beneficiaries contribute ?250?1000/month based on income; dependents include spouse, dependent parents, unmarried children up to 25. (Note: official domain changed from cghs.gov.in/cghs.nic.in to cghs.mohfw.gov.in in April 2025.)

name: Employees' State Insurance Scheme (ESIC/ESI)
max_income: Monthly wage ? ?21,000 (?25,000 for persons with disabilities)
benefits: Full medical care for insured person and family from day one of employment, no expenditure ceiling on treatment; ESIC sickness benefit at 70% of wages for up to 91 days/year; ESIC maternity benefit for 26 weeks at full wage
notes: Applies to establishments with 10+ employees (20+ in Maharashtra/Chandigarh); wage ceiling unchanged since January 2017.

name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
max_income: Net family income below ?8 lakh, or belonging to SC/ST, BPL, PMJAY beneficiaries, e-Shram/MGNREGA cardholders etc.
benefits: ?5,000 for first living child in two instalments; ?6,000 for second child if it is a girl, in one instalment after birth
notes: Excludes women in regular Central/State Govt or PSU employment or receiving similar statutory maternity benefits; stackable with JSY.

name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY)
max_income: BPL certification required in HPS states; simplified criteria where BPL cards unavailable
benefits: Rural institutional delivery: ?1,400; home delivery (BPL): ?500; National Health Mission amounts vary by state/LPS-HPS/rural-urban classification
notes: Covers up to two live births; women under 19 cannot register.

name: Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN) incl. Health Minister's Cancer Patient Fund
max_income: Annual family income should not exceed ?1,25,000
benefits: Up to ?15 lakh for life-threatening diseases, up to ?15 lakh under HMCPF for cancer, up to ?50 lakh for rare diseases
notes: Patient must belong to a BPL family; funds paid directly to the treating government hospital, not the patient; treatment only at government hospitals.

name: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP)
benefits: Generic medicines at 5090% cheaper prices than branded equivalents through 14,000+ Jan Aushadhi Kendras; sells 2,047+ medicines and 300+ surgical items
notes: Sanitary napkins available at ?1/pad; not an insurance scheme but a price-subsidy access program.

name: PM RAHAT (Road Accident Victim Hospitalization and Assured Treatment)
benefits: Cashless emergency medical treatment up to ?1.5 lakh per victim for road accident injuries, for the first 7 days from date of accident; stabilization guaranteed up to 24 hrs (non-life-threatening) or 48 hrs (life-threatening)
notes: Launched 13 Feb 2026; covers any road accident victim including hit-and-run cases; delivered via PM-JAY empanelled hospitals; integrated with ERSS-112.

name: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS)
max_income: NaduSources vary: ?72,000/year PolicyX in some, ?1,20,000/year Starhealth in others (figure has likely been revised over time  verify current threshold before relying on it
benefits: Up to ?5,00,000 per family per year on a floater basis; covers major surgeries, critical care, diagnostics, and follow-ups
notes: Sri Lankan refugees, interstate migrants (6+ months residence), and rescued/orphaned girl children also eligible.

name: HIMCARE (Himachal Health Care Scheme)
max_income: Below ?5 lakh annual family income (or BPL/white ration card holder)
benefits: ?5 lakh coverage per family; cashless hospitalisation at government and empanelled private hospitals in-state and a few out-of-state
notes: For families not covered under Ayushman Bharat; must not own more than 35 acres agricultural land; urban household must be under 3,000 sq.ft.

name: Sarbat Sehat Bima Yojana / Mukh Mantri Sehat Yojna (MMSY)
max_income: Originally below ?5 lakh (white ration card/BPL), Policybazaar but expanded to all bonafide Punjab households irrespective of income after January 2026 integration into MMSY
benefits: Coverage upgraded to ?10 lakh per family; covers 2,000 treatment packages; pre-existing disease coverage from Day 1 with no waiting period
notes: Integrated into MMSY in January 2026, broadening it beyond income-based eligibility.

name: Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres / Ayushman Arogya Mandir
benefits: Upgrading Sub Health Centres and PHCs to provide free basic checkups, screenings, and primary care closer to the community Press Information Bureau (target of 1,50,000 centres nationally)
notes: Formerly "Health & Wellness Centres," rebranded "Ayushman Arogya Mandir"; the primary-care pillar complementing the PM-JAY hospitalization pillar.
